meaning: n.(动物的)口鼻部分,脸,愁眉苦脸,势利者
examples: Tom biffed Fred on the snoot.
As the birds rise, the hunters snoot at them.
I saw him snoot into me street and disappeared.
Mr. Ferguson sensed that most Hollywood filmmakers were better at comedy than tragedy, and saw no reason to "snoot" their preference for the former.
Put your snoot in a glass of Barolo from a rich vintage, the kind people pay serious triple figures for, and see if "road tar" doesn't come close to the mark.
